titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Keith M. Jones Papers
titlestmt.author: Divinity Library Staff

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 8, 2025 at 18:15

did.abstract: This collection contains sermons and documentation of the life and work of a prominent Congregational minister in Connecticut who was active from 1940 to 1978. Substantial and comprehensive texts of sermons preached primarily in Wethersfield, CT provide an overview of issues and concerns of mainline American churches in the mid 20th century.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Keith Merwin Jones was born December 4, 1910, near Syracuse, Indiana, the son of Emeral Benton Jones and Maude Cory Jones. He received a B.A. from Manchester College in North Manchester, IN in 1932 and taught high school for two years in Wells County, IN before entering Garrett Theological Seminary, from which he received a B.D. in 1937. From 1937 to 1940 Jones completed the course work for his PhD at Yale University in philosophy and theology but did not finish the degree. After serving three and a half years as pastor of the Whitneyville Congregational Church in Hamden, CT he was called in 1943 to be minister of the First Church of Christ in Wethersfield where he served as senior minister until his retirement in June 1978. Jones was a prominent Congregational minister in Connecticut and was involved in the formation of the United Church of Christ in 1957, a merger of the Evangelical and Reformed Church and the General Council of the Congregational Christian Churches. Jones was chaplain to the Connecticut House of Representatives during 1961-1962. Jones married Lydia Marie Fox and they had three children: Wanda, Marcia, and Keith M., Jr.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The material is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Keith M. Jones family, 2021

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains sermons and documentation of the life and work of a prominent Congregational minister in Connecticut who was active from 1940 to 1978. Substantial and comprehensive texts of sermons preached primarily in Wethersfield, CT provide an overview of issues and concerns of mainline American churches in the mid 20th century. These papers complement other collections of Congregational clergy held by the Divinity Library, including the Henry David Gray papers.

Series I consists of a chronological sequence of sermons dating from 1941, shortly after Jones' graduation from Yale Divinity School, until his retirement in 1978. There are full texts for nearly all weeks during that span, including Sunday sermons and special services. The finding aid lists the first and last titles of the sermons in the date span contained in each folder. The sermons address both spiritual and societal concerns and shed light on the issues that a suburban church in Connecticut faced throughout this era.

Series II contains documentation related to the two churches where Jones served, Whitneyville Congregational Church in Hamden (1940-1943) and First Church of Christ in Wethersfield, CT (1943-1978). Besides acquiring a new parsonage for the Wethersfield church, Jones led three different building campaigns, two of them for additional parish buildings and one to renovate the interior of the 1761 meeting house. The church-related documentation in this series is supplemented by scrapbooks in Series III. Also included in the series are materials related to work in the larger Congregational and Connecticut scene, including with the Hartford Association of Congregational Christian Churches and Greater Hartford Council of Churches, and in connection with the merger that created the United Church of Christ in 1957.

Series III contains biographical documentation about Jones, his family, and his work. Notable are a set of circular letters and announcements that were sent to friends and family, primarily holiday letters but with other milestones documented as well. These provide a good overview of Jones' activities. Also available in this series is a set of correspondence with Julian Norris Hartt, a fellow graduate of Garrett and Yale, who was a lifelong friend and co-owner of a vacation property on Cape Cod. Scrapbooks in this series document activities at the churches where Jones worked.

Series IV, Collected Material, includes pamphlets and sermons by others that Jones collected as resources. Series V includes extensive audio tapes, primarily of sermons by Jones, as well as an array of family and church-related photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in five series:

did.unittitle: "For Him or Against Him" - "Our Congregational Heritage"
"Sufficient Unto the Day" - "Our Need for Wholeness"
"The Fellowship of the Table" - "For What are We Living?"
"The Center of Our Religion" - "Protestant Understanding of the Gospel"
"Cloud of Witnesses" - "Finding the Rest of Ourselves"
"Priesthood of All Believers" - "Living in All Three Dimensions"
"In the Valley of Vision" - "Big Things and Little"
"About Ordinary Saints Like You" - "Making the Most of Failure"
"Contagious Faith Within the Church" - "Isn't a Good Life Enough?"
"The Enough in Our Time" - "The Everlasting Light"
"Christ Enters the City" - "The Horizons of First Church"
Christmas Vesper Service - "New Lamps for Old"
"The Lord is Our Shepherd" - "Prisoners of Hope"
"Where is Freedom" - "Is the Past Good Enough for the Future?"
"We Are Drawn Together" - "Down to Earth Religion"
Maundy Thursday - "What Can We Bring Him?"
"In Him We Live and Move" - "Teach Me Thy Way"
"Anchors for the New Year" - "The Doubts of Our Faith"
"Is Miracle Enough?" - "It Is People Who Count"
"Lines of Communication" - "Now Thank We All One God"
"Hold Fast to What is Good" - "The Coming Festival"
"Eager to Believe the Best" - "Getting Our Spiritual Bearings"
"Partakers of One Bread" - "The God of Judgment"
"God of Grace" - "Measuring the Success of Life"
"To Our Comfort" - "Our Inadequacy and God's Sufficiency"
"Peace in the Midst of Tension" - "Heritage of the Reformation"
"Vessels of the Lord" - "God Works for Good"
"Forth Into a Larger Place" - "Faith in the Space Age"
"The People of God" - "The Lord is Always There"
"Do All Unto the Glory of God" - Christmas
"The Bread and the Cup" - "The Cross and the Flag"
"Our Reconciliation" - "Bound in the Bundle of Life"
"In Times of Emergency" - "Walking in the Reformers' Footsteps"
"Beyond this Life" - "At the Gateway of a New Year"
"The Divine Presence" - "From What Does Goodness Spring?"
"The End is Coming" - "The Lord is Risen"
"Thou Hast Made Us" - "In Times of Crisis"
"Called Into Community" - "Proclaim Liberty Throughout the Land"
"Unseen Battlefields" - "Our Larger Work in the UCC"
"Climate for Peace" - "National Day of Mourning for JFK"
"Christ Confronts; Points New Ways" - "On Being Religious"
"New Birth of Freedom" - Holy Week service
"Prayer Controversy in America" - "The Demonic in Life"
"God at the Wheel" - "Living in Controversial Situations"
Ash Wednesday - "Out of Death into Life"
"God of the Jet Age" - "Establish the Work of Our Hands"
"Wide as the World is Wide" - Thanksgiving Eve
"The Gap to be Closed" - "By Another Way"
"All the Marvelous Things" - "What Does This Uproar Mean?"
"Proclaim Liberty to All" - "Cradle of American Churches"
"The Common Cup" - "Mere Belief is Not Enough"
"A Look at the Minister" - UCC 10th Anniversary
"A Table Versus Walls" - "When Are We Being Religious?"
"The Now Gospel" - "God Makes Each One Different"
"Healing Within These Walls" - "Life's Inside and Outside"
"When God is Tossed Out, New Gods Crowd In" - "The Church is Born"
"Our Hand is On the Plow" - "Looking Ahead With Our Church"
"This Place of Our Worship" - "To Whom Much is Given"
"Born Free or Set Free?" - "Faith: Lotion or Lifeblood"
"Who Summons Whom?" - "Faith Does What Light Does"
"Trusting the Right Person" - "Windows of the Church"
"In Quietness and Trust Shall Be Your Strength" - "Outreach a Hundred Years Ago"
"The Church - New Appreciations" - "Our Kind of World"
"Lift Up Your Hands" - "Getting Lost and Being Found"
"Grace of God" - "The Stone Has Been Rolled Away"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thanksgiving, Independence Day, a copy of the Christian Century

did.unittitle: Whitneyville Congregational Church in Hamden, CT
Photos of the church building and people
Correspondence concerning Jones's transition from Whitneyville to First Church of Christ
First Church of Christ in Wethersfield, CT
Hartford Association of Congregational Christian Churches
Constitutions of the Hartford Association of Congregational Christian Churches
Jones's appointment to the Greater Hartford Council of Churches
Horace Bushnell Conference in Woodstock, NY
Essays, newsletters related to the merger
Documents related to racial discrimination in housing in Hartford
Photos, postcards, diary from a road trip
Bulletins from the Church of the Brethren

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: the church he grew up in

did.unittitle: Manchester College "A Century of Faith, Learning, and Service"
Teaching job between college and seminary
Garrett Biblical Institute memorabilia (diploma, grades, photos)
Materials described as "sermon ideas" (magazines, newspaper clippings, etc)
Biographical documentation and memorabilia related to Lydia (Fox) Jones
Publications mentioning Keith or the churches
Circular letters/announcements to family and friends
Circular letters/announcements to family and friends
Correspondence with Julian Norris Hartt (lifelong friend)
Assorted materials from his ministry at First Church of Christ Wethersfield

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photos, newspaper clippings, letters, other memborabilia

did.unittitle: Assorted materials from his ministry at First Church of Christ Wethersfield
History of the meeting house (handwritten by Keith)
Book written by friend Julian Hartt
Lydia Marie Fox Jones's childhood and young adulthood
Large photo of the CT legislature
